purpose: Consistent import order keeps diffs small, makes merges predictable, and reduces cognitive load when scanning a file. Without a shared rule, contributors reach for different defaults (alphabetical, dependency order, by category) and the codebase churns.

references.description: decision to adopt the black profile for isort

rules.statement: Group imports in three blocks separated by a single blank line: standard library, third-party, first-party.
Sort imports alphabetically within each group.
Place `import x` statements before `from x import y` statements within the same group.
Do not use wildcard imports (`from x import *`) outside of `__init__.py` re-exports.
Run `isort` with the `black` profile as part of pre-commit.
